briefdescription.para: Opaque type to store regulator DVS states.
Opaque type to store regulator modes.
Opaque bit map for regulator error flags (see REGULATOR_ERRORS)

memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, const struct regulator_event *const evt, const void *const user_data)
memberdef.name: regulator_callback_t

detaileddescription.para: Reference-counted request that a regulator be turned on. A regulator is considered "on" when it has reached a stable/usable state. Regulators that are always on, or configured in devicetree with regulator-always-on will always stay enabled, and so this function will always succeed.

parameterdescription.para: If regulator has been successfully enabled.
Negative errno in case of failure.
If regulator enablement can not be controlled.

briefdescription.para: Check if a regulator is enabled.

detaileddescription.para: Release a regulator after a previous regulator_enable() completed successfully. Regulators that are always on, or configured in devicetree with regulator-always-on will always stay enabled, and so this function will always succeed.
This must be invoked at most once for each successful regulator_enable().

parameterdescription.para: If regulator has been successfully disabled.
Negative errno in case of failure.
If regulator disablement can not be controlled.

briefdescription.para: Obtain the number of supported voltage levels.

detaileddescription.para: Each voltage level supported by a regulator gets an index, starting from zero. The total number of supported voltage levels can be used together with regulator_list_voltage() to list all supported voltage levels.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, unsigned int idx, int32_t *volt_uv)
memberdef.name: regulator_list_voltage

briefdescription.para: Obtain the value of a voltage given an index.

detaileddescription.para: Each voltage level supported by a regulator gets an index, starting from zero. Together with regulator_count_voltages(), this function can be used to iterate over all supported voltages.

parameterdescription.para: Where voltage for the given index will be stored, in microvolts.
If index corresponds to a supported voltage.
If index does not correspond to a supported voltage.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, int32_t min_uv, int32_t max_uv)
memberdef.name: regulator_is_supported_voltage

briefdescription.para: Check if a voltage within a window is supported.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, int32_t min_uv, int32_t max_uv)
memberdef.name: regulator_set_voltage

detaileddescription.para: The output voltage will be configured to the closest supported output voltage. regulator_get_voltage() can be used to obtain the actual configured voltage. The voltage will be applied to the active or selected mode. Output voltage may be limited using regulator-min-microvolt and/or regulator-max-microvolt in devicetree.

parameterdescription.para: If the given voltage window is not valid.
In case of any other error.
Where configured output voltage will be stored.
In case of any other error.

briefdescription.para: Obtain the number of supported current limit levels.

detaileddescription.para: Each current limit level supported by a regulator gets an index, starting from zero. The total number of supported current limit levels can be used together with regulator_list_current_limit() to list all supported current limit levels.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, unsigned int idx, int32_t *current_ua)
memberdef.name: regulator_list_current_limit

briefdescription.para: Obtain the value of a current limit given an index.

detaileddescription.para: Each current limit level supported by a regulator gets an index, starting from zero. Together with regulator_count_current_limits(), this function can be used to iterate over all supported current limits.

parameterdescription.para: Where current for the given index will be stored, in microamps.
If index corresponds to a supported current limit.
If index does not correspond to a supported current limit.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, int32_t min_ua, int32_t max_ua)
memberdef.name: regulator_set_current_limit

detaileddescription.para: The output current limit will be configured to the closest supported output current limit. regulator_get_current_limit() can be used to obtain the actual configured current limit. Current may be limited using current-min-microamp and/or current-max-microamp in Devicetree.

parameterdescription.para: Minimum acceptable current limit in microamps.
Maximum acceptable current limit in microamps.
If the given current limit window is not valid.
In case of any other error.
Where output current limit will be stored.
In case of any other error.

detaileddescription.para: Regulators can support multiple modes in order to permit different voltage configuration or better power savings. This API will apply a mode for the regulator. Allowed modes may be limited using regulator-allowed-modes devicetree property.

parameterdescription.para: Mode to select for this regulator.
In case of any other error.
In case of any other error.
In case of any other error.
Where active discharge will be stored.
In case of any other error.
Where error flags will be stored.
In case of any other error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, regulator_callback_t cb, const void *const user_data)
memberdef.name: regulator_set_callback

detaileddescription.para: The regulator may generate an interrupt when, for example, the voltage is present. This can be used for USB VBUS voltage regulators to notify that a device is connected to a port.

parameterdescription.para: If not supported by the device.
